perturbation_predictions.expected_behavior: Removing state A makes the metamorphic mechanism partial or invalid.
perturbation_predictions.falsifiable_if: one state explains all XCL1 behavior

perturbation_predictions.expected_behavior: Removing state B makes the metamorphic mechanism partial or invalid.
perturbation_predictions.falsifiable_if: one state explains all XCL1 behavior

perturbation_predictions.expected_behavior: State-specific function labels must remain separated.
perturbation_predictions.falsifiable_if: state functions can be merged without weakening the mechanism

perturbation_predictions.falsifiable_if: balance shifts leave the two-state mechanism unchanged

scientist_question: Can the grammar identify that perturbing state separation or deleting one state destroys the mechanism?
